response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior software craftsman with strong full-stack and systems background, but the role specifically targets a C++ developer with 8-12 years building high-performance C++/STL applications, IoT/MQTT, secure development per IEC 62443, and industrial automation experience. The resume shows only a brief 7-month stint writing C/C++ at National Instruments back in 2014-2015; the bulk of the career is in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/GIS platforms. Core required competencies (modern C++, message brokers, MQTT, IEC 62443/SDL, industrial protocols) are absent.
strengths: 10+ years of senior-level software engineering with strong craftsmanship, TDD, and CI/CD practice
Distributed/event-driven systems experience (Sociomantic micro-services, GISCollective event-driven platform)
Node.js, TypeScript, and Python-adjacent scripting align with the 'advantageous' stack
concerns: Minimal and dated C++ experience (~7 months in 2014-2015); no modern C++/STL portfolio
No evidence of MQTT, message brokers, IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial automation/IoT protocols
Career trajectory is web/full-stack focused, not the high-performance on-prem/cloud C++ platform work the role centers on

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity / IEC 62443 / SDL background
Strong general engineering seniority and distributed-systems exposure
